projects.name: Postgres Migration
projects.description: Migration from Oracle 19c to PostgreSQL 16 for the core transaction platform

projects.name: API Gateway Modernisation
projects.description: Replace legacy MuleSoft gateway with Kong-based API Gateway

projects.name: Zero Trust Security Framework
projects.description: Implement Zero Trust network architecture across all production systems

projects.name: Data Platform Modernisation
projects.description: Replace on-premise Hadoop cluster with cloud-native Databricks lakehouse

projects.name: Customer Portal Redesign
projects.description: Full redesign of customer-facing portal using React and micro-frontends

context_objects.title: JIRA-1042: Postgres Migration — Schema Conversion for Orders Table
context_objects.content: Task: Convert the ORDERS table schema from Oracle to PostgreSQL. Completed schema translation for 847 columns across 23 tables. Key changes: replaced NUMBER(38) with NUMERIC, CLOB with TEXT, DATE with TIMESTAMPTZ. Sequence objects replaced with IDENTITY columns. Discovered 3 Oracle-specific PL/SQL functions with no direct PG equivalent — raised as blockers JIRA-1055, JIRA-1056, JIRA-1057. Current status: 18/23 tables migrated. Estimated completion: Sprint 14.

context_objects.title: JIRA-1043: Implement Connection Pooling for PG Cluster
context_objects.content: Implement PgBouncer as connection pooler in front of the PostgreSQL cluster. Target: 3-node primary-replica setup with PgBouncer in transaction mode. Pool size: 100 connections per node. Rationale: Legacy Oracle connection model allowed unlimited connections; PostgreSQL recommends max_connections=500 for our workload. PgBouncer will handle up to 10,000 application connections. Status: PgBouncer deployed in staging. Load tests passing at 8,000 concurrent connections. Production deployment planned for Sprint 13, Week 2.

context_objects.title: JIRA-1044: Data Validation Framework for Migration Cutover
context_objects.content: Design and implement automated data validation framework to verify data integrity post-migration. Approach: Row count comparison, checksum validation on critical tables, business rule validation via sample queries. Tooling: Custom Python scripts using SQLAlchemy + pytest. Scope: 127 critical tables identified, validation scripts written for 89. Findings: 3 tables show row count discrepancy — under investigation. All financial tables require CRC32 checksum validation before sign-off. Timeline: Validation complete by Sprint 13 end.

context_objects.title: JIRA-1045: Cutover Runbook — Production Migration Weekend
context_objects.content: Cutover runbook for Oracle-to-PostgreSQL production migration. Cutover window: Saturday 23:00 UTC to Sunday 08:00 UTC. Steps: (1) Enable maintenance mode on all APIs — 10min. (2) Final Oracle export dump — 45min. (3) Transfer and restore to PG primary — 90min. (4) Run validation suite — 60min. (5) Update application connection strings — 20min. (6) Smoke tests — 30min. (7) Go/no-go decision — 15min. (8) Disable maintenance mode or rollback. Rollback procedure: Revert DNS CNAME to Oracle endpoint; estimated rollback time 25 minutes. Stakeholders: Elena Vasquez (decision authority), Priya Sharma (security sign-off).

context_objects.title: JIRA-2001: API Gateway — Replace MuleSoft Rate Limiting
context_objects.content: Migrate rate limiting rules from MuleSoft to Kong API Gateway. Current state: 142 rate limiting policies in MuleSoft, varying by client tier (Free/Pro/Enterprise). Kong implementation: using kong-plugin-rate-limiting with Redis backend. Progress: 128/142 policies migrated. Remaining 14 are custom Groovy scripts with complex business logic — requires re-implementation in Lua. ETA: 2 more sprints. Risk: MuleSoft licence expires in 6 weeks — hard deadline.

context_objects.title: JIRA-2002: Kong Plugin Development — JWT Authentication
context_objects.content: Develop custom Kong plugin for JWT authentication aligned with our internal OIDC provider (Keycloak). Plugin must: validate RS256 JWT signatures, extract custom claims (org_id, user_tier), enforce audience restrictions. Implementation: Lua plugin, tested with Newman/Postman. Status: Plugin complete, passing 47/47 test cases in staging. Pending security review from Priya Sharma. Deployment blocked until security review complete (ETA: next week).

context_objects.title: JIRA-3001: Zero Trust — Network Segmentation Design
context_objects.content: Design microsegmentation rules for Zero Trust implementation. Approach: Calico CNI for Kubernetes-level policy enforcement. Each service namespace gets deny-all default policy. Explicit allow rules for each service-to-service communication path. Current: 89 services mapped, 234 communication paths documented, 189 policies drafted. Challenge: Legacy monolith has undocumented internal dependencies — network tracing with Cilium Hubble reveals 47 additional flows. Status: In progress, expected completion Sprint 15.

context_objects.title: JIRA-4001: Databricks Cluster Provisioning
context_objects.content: Provision Databricks workspace and compute clusters for data platform migration. Environment: Azure Databricks Premium. Cluster configuration: Standard_DS3_v2 nodes, autoscaling 2-16 workers. Unity Catalog enabled for governance. Status: Dev and staging workspaces provisioned. Production provisioning requires InfoSec sign-off (tracking in JIRA-4002). Delta Lake tables created for first 12 source datasets. Estimated data migration: 47TB first tranche.

context_objects.title: JIRA-5001: Customer Portal — Design System Integration
context_objects.content: Integrate Storybook-based design system into React micro-frontend architecture. Design system: 87 shared components, using Tailwind CSS + Radix UI. Module Federation setup allows each micro-frontend to consume shared components without bundle duplication. Status: 6 of 8 micro-frontend teams migrated to new design system. 2 teams blocked on legacy AngularJS dependencies — tracked separately. Performance: Lighthouse score improved from 61 to 84 after bundle optimisation.

context_objects.title: JIRA-1050: Postgres Migration — Performance Benchmarking Results
context_objects.content: Performance benchmark results comparing Oracle vs PostgreSQL query performance. Test: 500 critical production queries executed against both systems with production data clone. Results: 87% of queries within 5% of Oracle baseline. 9% showing 10-25% improvement (optimizer differences). 4% showing degradation: ORDERS_BY_REGION query 340ms vs Oracle 87ms — requires index tuning. Partitioning strategy for EVENTS table (2.3B rows) reduces query time from 45s to 3.2s. Recommendation: Proceed to cutover with 3 queries flagged for post-migration optimisation.

context_objects.title: INC-4521: Production Database Outage — Connection Pool Exhaustion
context_objects.content: CRITICAL INCIDENT — Duration: 47 minutes. Root cause: PgBouncer misconfiguration caused connection pool exhaustion during Black Friday peak load. pool_mode was set to 'session' instead of 'transaction', causing connections to be held for full session duration. At 14:23 UTC, connection pool reached 500/500. New requests started queuing, API response times exceeded 30s timeout, triggering cascading failures across Order Processing, Inventory, and Payment services. Mitigation: Emergency restart of PgBouncer with corrected transaction-mode config. Post-incident: Added pool utilization alerting at 70% threshold. ADR-007 updated with pool configuration requirements.

context_objects.title: INC-4498: API Gateway Timeout Cascade — MuleSoft Memory Leak
context_objects.content: HIGH severity incident. Duration: 2h 14min. Root cause: Memory leak in MuleSoft Anypoint Runtime caused JVM heap exhaustion after 72 hours of uptime. Symptoms: Gradual API response time degradation starting at 08:15 UTC, full gateway failure at 09:47 UTC. Impact: All API consumers affected, estimated 12,000 failed transactions. Mitigation: Rolling restart of MuleSoft nodes (4 nodes, 15min each). Permanent fix: Increased JVM heap from 4GB to 8GB, added automated weekly restart job. Root cause analysis confirmed memory retained in custom DataWeave transformation caches. Fix: Clear cache on request completion. This incident reinforces priority of MuleSoft to Kong migration.

context_objects.title: INC-4512: Security Breach Attempt — Lateral Movement Detected
context_objects.content: HIGH severity security incident. Detection: Unusual cross-namespace traffic detected by Cilium Hubble at 03:17 UTC. A compromised staging pod attempted lateral movement to production namespace. Zero Trust policies blocked the attempt — 0 production systems accessed. Post-incident: Staging environment isolated, compromised credentials rotated, forensic analysis completed. Finding: Build pipeline token with excessive permissions was the initial compromise vector. Remediation: Pipeline tokens scoped to minimum required permissions, secret rotation policy automated to 90-day cycle. This incident validates the Zero Trust investment.

context_objects.title: INC-4489: Data Pipeline Failure — Oracle CDC Interruption
context_objects.content: MEDIUM severity incident. The Oracle Change Data Capture (CDC) pipeline feeding the data warehouse experienced 6-hour interruption. Root cause: LogMiner archive log retention set to 24h — logs rolled before pipeline recovery after planned maintenance. Impact: 6-hour gap in data warehouse, downstream reports delayed. Mitigation: Replay from Oracle backup logs (successful). Prevention: Log retention extended to 7 days. Note: This incident is moot post-Postgres migration as PostgreSQL logical replication (pglogical) does not have this constraint.

context_objects.title: INC-4530: Customer Portal — Authentication Token Expiry Bug
context_objects.content: LOW severity production bug. JWT tokens were expiring 30 minutes earlier than expected due to clock skew between frontend servers and Keycloak. Symptoms: Users were unexpectedly logged out mid-session. Impact: ~340 customers affected over 4 hours. Root cause: NTP synchronisation disabled on 3 portal frontend nodes during a recent kernel upgrade. Fix: NTP re-enabled, tokens now verified with 5-minute clock skew tolerance (nbf/exp validation). Post-fix: Zero customer reports in 72-hour monitoring period.

context_objects.title: ADR-007: PostgreSQL as Primary OLTP Database
context_objects.content: Status: Accepted. Context: The organisation's Oracle 19c licence expires in Q3 2026. Cost analysis shows 73% reduction in database licensing costs by migrating to PostgreSQL. PostgreSQL 16 now provides enterprise-grade features including logical replication, row-level security, and partitioning that meet our OLTP requirements. Decision: Adopt PostgreSQL 16 as the primary OLTP database for all transaction-processing workloads. Consequences: (1) All Oracle-specific features (CONNECT BY, analytic functions) must be rewritten. (2) DBA team upskilling required — budget approved. (3) Migration tooling: Ora2Pg for schema, custom Python for data validation. (4) No new Oracle-dependent features to be built from this date. Supersedes ADR-003.

context_objects.title: ADR-012: Kong API Gateway as API Management Platform
context_objects.content: Status: Accepted. Context: MuleSoft Anypoint Platform licence cost is $1.2M/year. Kong Gateway (OSS + Enterprise) provides equivalent functionality at $180K/year. MuleSoft's Java-based runtime has shown memory leak issues (INC-4498). Decision: Adopt Kong Gateway as the organisation's API management platform. All new API policies to be written as Kong plugins (Lua). Existing MuleSoft policies to be migrated before licence expiry (Q2 2026). Consequences: Team must develop Lua plugin development capability. Kong's declarative configuration (deck) enables GitOps for API policies. Supersedes ADR-008.

context_objects.title: ADR-015: Zero Trust Network Architecture
context_objects.content: Status: Accepted. Context: Following INC-4512 (lateral movement attempt), the security perimeter model is proven insufficient. The organisation operates across 3 cloud providers and 2 on-premise data centres — traditional VPN-based perimeter is unmanageable. Decision: Implement Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA) as defined in NIST SP 800-207. Core principles: (1) Verify explicitly — authenticate and authorise every request. (2) Use least privilege access — JIT/JEA, risk-based adaptive policies. (3) Assume breach — segment networks, verify end-to-end encryption, use analytics to detect anomalies. Implementation: Calico for network policy, Istio service mesh for mTLS, Keycloak for identity, Vault for secrets. Timeline: 18-month programme.

context_objects.title: ADR-016: Databricks Lakehouse as Data Platform
context_objects.content: Status: Accepted. Context: On-premise Hadoop cluster (72 nodes, 5 years old) requires $4.2M hardware refresh. TCO analysis favours cloud migration. Decision: Migrate to Azure Databricks with Delta Lake as the unified analytics platform. Rationale: (1) Delta Lake provides ACID transactions for data lake. (2) Unity Catalog for unified data governance. (3) MLflow integration for ML lifecycle management. (4) 60% cost reduction vs Hadoop refresh. Consequences: Hadoop skills become redundant — retraining programme required. Data gravity concern: 47TB initial dataset — data transfer costs estimated $12K.

context_objects.title: ADR-018: React Micro-Frontend Architecture for Customer Portal
context_objects.content: Status: Accepted. Context: Monolithic Angular 10 customer portal has 340,000 LOC. Deployment frequency: 1x per month (all teams blocked on shared release). Decision: Decompose into 8 micro-frontends using Webpack Module Federation. Each micro-frontend owned by a dedicated team with independent deployment pipeline. Shell application handles routing and shared state. Shared design system consumed as a federated remote. Consequences: Teams gain deployment autonomy — target frequency 2x per week per team. Cold load performance must be managed via code splitting and lazy loading. Shared state management via Zustand (replacing NgRx).

context_objects.title: ADR-019: PgBouncer Transaction-Mode Connection Pooling
context_objects.content: Status: Accepted. Context: Following INC-4521, explicit configuration guidance for PgBouncer is required to prevent recurrence. Decision: PgBouncer MUST be configured in transaction pooling mode for all application connections. Session pooling is explicitly prohibited for high-throughput services. Configuration requirements: pool_mode=transaction, max_client_conn=5000, default_pool_size=100, reserve_pool_size=20, reserve_pool_timeout=3. Monitoring: Pool utilisation alert at 70%, critical at 85%. Runbook for pool exhaustion documented in runbook-db-001. All new PostgreSQL deployments must include this configuration validated in the deployment checklist.

context_objects.title: Architecture Document: Postgres Migration Technical Design
context_objects.content: Version 2.3 — Technical Design for Oracle to PostgreSQL Migration. Scope: 23 Oracle schemas, 847 tables, 2.1TB data, 127 stored procedures. Architecture: Source: Oracle 19c RAC (2-node) → Target: PostgreSQL 16 (1 primary + 2 read replicas). Migration tooling: Ora2Pg for schema conversion, custom Python validation framework, PgBouncer for connection pooling, pglogical for CDC post-cutover. Network architecture: Application tier connects via PgBouncer (port 5432). Read replicas serve analytics workloads via read-only pool (port 5433). High availability: Patroni for automatic failover, HAProxy for connection routing. Backup strategy: pg_basebackup daily, WAL archiving to S3 (5-min RPO). Monitoring: Prometheus + Grafana, custom dashboards for replication lag, query performance, pool utilisation.

context_objects.title: Architecture Document: Zero Trust Reference Architecture
context_objects.content: Version 1.4 — Zero Trust Reference Architecture for Enterprise Systems. Principles: Following NIST SP 800-207. Layers: (1) Identity layer: Keycloak OIDC + SAML, MFA enforced for all human identities, SPIFFE/SPIRE for workload identity. (2) Network layer: Calico network policies (deny-all default), Istio service mesh with mTLS, Cilium Hubble for observability. (3) Application layer: OPA (Open Policy Agent) for authorisation decisions, JWT validation at API Gateway. (4) Data layer: Column-level encryption for PII, PostgreSQL row-level security. (5) Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault with dynamic secrets, automatic rotation. Monitoring: SIEM integration with Splunk, anomaly detection via ML model trained on baseline traffic patterns.

context_objects.title: Sprint 13 Report — Platform Engineering
context_objects.content: Sprint 13 (May 6–May 19, 2026). Team: Platform Engineering. Velocity: 89 points (target: 85). Completed: JIRA-1042 schema conversion (23pts), JIRA-1043 PgBouncer deployment (21pts), JIRA-1050 performance benchmarking (18pts), JIRA-1044 validation framework (27pts). Carried over: JIRA-1045 cutover runbook (13pts) — 70% complete. Blockers: 3 Oracle PL/SQL functions awaiting conversion (JIRA-1055, 1056, 1057). Key risks: Cutover window narrowing — must complete by Sprint 14 or risk Oracle licence extension costs ($240K/month). Team health: 2 members on PTO next sprint — velocity forecast reduced to 70pts.

context_objects.title: Sprint 12 Report — Backend Engineering
context_objects.content: Sprint 12 (April 22–May 5, 2026). Team: Backend Engineering. Velocity: 76 points (target: 80). Completed: JIRA-2001 rate limiting migration 128/142 policies (34pts), JIRA-2002 JWT plugin development (42pts). Carried over: Remaining 14 MuleSoft policies (20pts) — complex Groovy logic requiring Lua rewrite. Blockers: JIRA-2002 blocked on security review. Risk: MuleSoft licence expiry creates hard 6-week deadline. Dependencies: Security team review required by May 15. Architecture review of Kong plugin by Elena Vasquez (scheduled May 22). Next sprint: Complete policy migration, begin Kong deployment to staging.

context_objects.title: Sprint 11 Report — Security & Compliance
context_objects.content: Sprint 11 (April 8–April 21, 2026). Team: Security & Compliance. Velocity: 68 points. Completed: Network topology mapping (34pts), Calico policy drafting for 189 services (34pts). Highlights: INC-4512 post-incident remediation completed — all recommendations implemented. Zero Trust maturity assessment: Current state 2.1/5.0, target 4.0/5.0 by Q4. Key dependency: Network segmentation requires Kubernetes cluster upgrade to 1.30 (Platform Engineering backlog). Risk: Legacy monolith has 47 undocumented service dependencies — discovery taking longer than estimated. Next sprint: Complete Calico policy implementation, begin Istio mTLS rollout.

context_objects.title: Sprint 10 Report — Data Engineering
context_objects.content: Sprint 10 (March 25–April 7, 2026). Team: Data Engineering. Velocity: 54 points (reduced — INC-4489 recovery consumed 18pts). Completed: Databricks workspace provisioning for dev/staging (36pts). In progress: Oracle CDC pipeline stabilisation (18pts carried). Highlights: Delta Lake tables created for 12 source datasets — data quality checks passing. Risk: Production provisioning blocked pending InfoSec review of Azure Databricks data residency requirements. 47TB initial data transfer estimated at 14 days continuous upload — network bandwidth constraint. Next sprint: Complete CDC stabilisation, begin ETL job migration.

context_objects.title: Meeting Notes: Postgres Migration Steering Committee — May 21
context_objects.content: Attendees: Priya Sharma (Director, chair), Elena Vasquez (Architect), Yuki Tanaka (Lead), Marcus Chen (Lead), James Okonkwo (Architect). Agenda: Cutover readiness review. Key discussion points: (1) Performance benchmarks reviewed — 4 queries flagged, Elena confident they can be addressed post-cutover without user impact. (2) Validation framework: 3 tables with row count discrepancy — Marcus confirmed these are known shadow tables no longer used by applications, validation rules updated. (3) Rollback procedure reviewed and approved by all stakeholders. (4) GO decision: Priya approved cutover for May 31 weekend window, subject to: validation suite achieving 100% pass rate by May 28. Actions: Elena to update runbook with final timings. Yuki to prepare on-call roster.

context_objects.title: Meeting Notes: API Gateway Architecture Review — May 14
context_objects.content: Attendees: Elena Vasquez (Architect), Marcus Chen (Lead), Fatima Al-Hassan (Engineer), Priya Sharma (Director). Purpose: Architecture review of Kong JWT plugin before production deployment. Discussion: (1) Security review findings: Priya raised concern about token revocation — current implementation checks JWT signature but not revocation list. Action: Add Redis-backed token revocation check with 5-min cache TTL. (2) Plugin architecture approved as Kong custom plugin pattern. (3) Deployment strategy: Blue-green deployment via Kong Canary plugin — 10% traffic initially, full cutover after 24h monitoring. Decision: Plugin approved for staging deployment. Production deployment after revocation fix (ETA: 1 week). Next review: May 28.

context_objects.title: Meeting Notes: Zero Trust Quarterly Review — Q1 2026
context_objects.content: Attendees: Priya Sharma (Director), David Kowalski (Engineer), Yuki Tanaka (Lead). Maturity assessment: Current score 2.1/5. Progress: Network segmentation 65% complete, identity layer 85% complete. Risks discussed: (1) Legacy monolith cannot run in strict Zero Trust mode without significant refactoring — decision: legacy monolith to remain in isolated VLAN with enhanced monitoring until refactoring complete (ADR-020 to be drafted). (2) Third-party SaaS integrations require whitelist exceptions — 23 SaaS tools requiring IP-based bypass rules. Budget: Programme on track, Q1 spend $420K of $2.1M approved budget. Next milestone: Complete network layer by Q2 end.

context_objects.title: Meeting Notes: Data Platform Migration Kickoff
context_objects.content: Attendees: James Okonkwo (Architect), Roberto Silva (Engineer), Yuki Tanaka (Lead), Finance stakeholder. Purpose: Migration programme kickoff. Scope confirmed: 47TB initial migration, 200+ ETL jobs to be rewritten in PySpark/Databricks notebooks. Timeline: 12-month programme. Phase 1 (Q1-Q2): Dev/staging provisioning and proof-of-concept. Phase 2 (Q3): Production provisioning and first 50 ETL jobs. Phase 3 (Q4): Full migration and Hadoop decommission. Finance approved $1.8M budget for Year 1. Key risk: ETL rewrite complexity — some jobs have undocumented business logic. Action: Roberto to audit all ETL jobs and produce complexity matrix.

context_objects.title: Email: Oracle Licence Extension Request — URGENT
context_objects.content: From: Elena Vasquez To: Priya Sharma, CFO. Subject: URGENT — Oracle Licence Extension Decision Required. The Oracle 19c database licence expires on 2026-08-31. If the Postgres Migration does not complete before this date, we require a licence extension at $240,000/month. Based on current sprint velocity and the cutover scheduled for May 31, we have adequate buffer. However, if any of the following blockers materialise, we will require an extension: (1) PL/SQL conversion not completed (3 functions outstanding). (2) Validation suite fails on cutover weekend. (3) Performance regression identified requiring extended tuning. Requesting written authorisation to proceed with cutover on May 31, or if board risk appetite requires the safety of an extension, to approve the $240K/month contingency. Decision required by May 24.

context_objects.title: Email: Security Policy Update — JWT Token Expiry Standards
context_objects.content: From: Priya Sharma To: All Engineering Teams. Subject: Updated JWT Token Expiry and Clock Skew Policy (effective immediately). Following INC-4530, the following standards are now mandatory for all JWT implementations: (1) Access token expiry: 15 minutes maximum. (2) Refresh token expiry: 8 hours for interactive sessions, 1 hour for service accounts. (3) Clock skew tolerance: Configure nbf and exp validation with ±5 minute tolerance. (4) NTP synchronisation: All servers must sync to internal NTP servers (ntp1.internal, ntp2.internal) every 5 minutes — verify in deployment checklist. (5) Token revocation: High-sensitivity endpoints must check token revocation list (Redis-backed) on every request. Non-compliance will be flagged in next security audit.

context_objects.title: Git Commit: feat(migration): implement PgBouncer transaction pooling config
context_objects.content: Commit: a7f3c2d. Author: Arjun Patel. Branch: feature/pgbouncer-config. Message: feat(migration): implement PgBouncer transaction pooling config per ADR-019. Changes: pgbouncer/pgbouncer.ini updated — pool_mode changed from session to transaction, max_client_conn increased to 5000, default_pool_size set to 100. Added Prometheus metrics exporter (pgbouncer_exporter) configuration. Grafana dashboard added: dashboards/pgbouncer-overview.json. Tests: integration/test_connection_pool.py — 12 new tests covering pool exhaustion, reconnection, and transaction isolation. All tests passing. Closes INC-4521 remediation.

context_objects.title: Git Commit: feat(kong): jwt-plugin with RS256 validation and revocation
context_objects.content: Commit: b9d1e8f. Author: Fatima Al-Hassan. Branch: feature/kong-jwt-plugin. Message: feat(kong): implement JWT plugin with RS256 validation, custom claim extraction, and Redis revocation list. Changes: kong/plugins/jwt-enterprise/handler.lua — full JWT validation with RS256, extracts org_id and user_tier claims. kong/plugins/jwt-enterprise/schema.lua — plugin configuration schema. kong/plugins/jwt-enterprise/revocation.lua — Redis-backed token revocation check with 5-min cache TTL. Tests: spec/jwt_plugin_spec.lua — 47 test cases. All passing. Addresses security review feedback from meeting 2026-05-14. Resolves JIRA-2002 blocker.

context_objects.title: Security Policy: Production Database Access Control
context_objects.content: Policy ID: SEC-DB-001. Version: 2.1. Effective: 2026-01-01. Scope: All production database systems. Requirements: (1) No direct database access from developer workstations — all queries via approved tooling (DBeaver with MFA, pgAdmin via bastion). (2) Database credentials must be stored in HashiCorp Vault — no hardcoded credentials in application code or configuration files. (3) Database users must follow least-privilege principle — application users have no DDL privileges. (4) All administrative actions must be performed via Ansible playbooks with full audit trail. (5) Connection pooling mandatory for all OLTP workloads (see ADR-019). (6) Encryption in transit: TLS 1.3 minimum for all database connections. Violation reporting: security@enterprise.com. Review cycle: Annual.

context_objects.title: Sprint 14 Report — Platform Engineering
context_objects.content: Sprint 14 (May 20–June 2, 2026). Team: Platform Engineering. Velocity: 71 points (reduced due to 2 members on PTO). Completed: JIRA-1045 cutover runbook (finalised, 13pts), JIRA-1055/1056/1057 PL/SQL conversion (28pts), final validation suite run (validation 127/127 tables passing, 30pts). Highlights: All cutover blockers resolved. Validation suite achieving 100% pass rate as required by steering committee. Cutover approved for May 31 weekend. On-call roster confirmed: Yuki Tanaka (primary), Elena Vasquez (escalation), Arjun Patel (database), Marcus Chen (application). Risk: Weather forecast for datacenter region shows risk of power instability — UPS tested and confirmed operational.

context_objects.title: Meeting Notes: Pre-Cutover Go/No-Go Call — May 30
context_objects.content: Attendees: Priya Sharma (Director, decision authority), Elena Vasquez (Architect), Yuki Tanaka (Lead), Arjun Patel (Database Engineer), Marcus Chen (Application Lead). Validation suite: 127/127 tables PASS. Performance benchmarks: 500 queries within tolerance. PgBouncer: Staging load test passed at 9,500 concurrent connections. Rollback: Procedure tested in staging — rollback time 22 minutes (within 25min target). Stakeholder sign-off: All present stakeholders confirmed GO. Communications: Customer notification sent for 23:00–08:00 maintenance window. On-call escalation path confirmed. DECISION: GO for cutover on May 31 23:00 UTC. Monitoring dashboard: monitoring.internal/postgres-cutover.

context_objects.title: Meeting Notes: API Gateway Deployment Review — May 28
context_objects.content: Attendees: Elena Vasquez, Marcus Chen, Fatima Al-Hassan, Priya Sharma. JWT plugin review: Token revocation fix verified — Redis integration tested under 5,000 req/sec load, 99.9% cache hit rate. Deployment plan reviewed: Canary deployment — 10% traffic on June 2, monitoring for 24h, full cutover June 3. Rollback plan: DNS TTL set to 60s for rapid rollback. Metrics to monitor: Error rate, p99 latency, token validation failures, revocation cache miss rate. Priya approved production deployment pending 24h canary success. Action: Marcus to update JIRA-2001 status to 'Ready for Production'.

context_objects.title: INC-4535: Postgres Cutover — Unexpected Replication Lag
context_objects.content: HIGH severity issue during cutover window (May 31, 02:47 UTC). During step 3 (restore to PG primary), replication lag to replica nodes reached 45 minutes — exceeding acceptable threshold of 10 minutes. Root cause: WAL generation rate during restore exceeded replica apply rate due to network bandwidth saturation on replica link. Mitigation: Increased wal_sender_timeout, adjusted wal_compression=on, throttled restore rate. Replication caught up by 04:15 UTC. Impact: Cutover window extended by 90 minutes — still within rollback deadline. Go decision confirmed at 04:30 UTC. Lesson learned: Future cutover runbooks must include replication lag monitoring thresholds and pre-approved mitigation steps.
